metadata.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
metadata.description: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 is the international standard for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS). It specifies requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an ISMS within the context of the organization. The 2022 revision restructured the Annex A controls into 4 themes with 93 controls total, replacing the previous 14-domain structure with 114 controls.
metadata.source_organization: International Organization for Standardization (ISO) / International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
metadata.classification_reasoning: ISO 27001 is a certification framework requiring formal audits by accredited Certification Bodies (CBs) under accreditation bodies such as UKAS, ANAB, or JAS-ANZ. Organizations achieve certification through Stage 1 (documentation review) and Stage 2 (implementation audit) assessments.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an information security management system (ISMS), ensuring the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets through a risk-based approach.
metadata.framework_scope: Applicable to all types and sizes of organizations seeking to establish and maintain an ISMS, manage information security risks, and demonstrate conformity to information security requirements.
framework_objectives: Establish a systematic approach to managing sensitive company information
Ensure confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information
Achieve continuous improvement of the ISMS
Demonstrate compliance with legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, Information Security Managers, IT Governance Teams, Risk Managers, Compliance Officers, Internal Auditors, and Senior Management responsible for information security
metadata.certification_body: Accredited Certification Bodies (e.g., BSI, Bureau Veritas, TÜV, DNV, SGS, Schellman)
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Loss of certification, reputational damage, potential loss of business contracts requiring ISO 27001 certification, and inability to demonstrate due diligence in information security management.
adoption_approach: Conduct internal audits and management reviews
Engage accredited CB for Stage 1 and Stage 2 audits
Maintain through surveillance audits and continual improvement

controls.title: Policies for Information Security
controls.description: A set of policies for information security shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals or if significant changes occur.
controls.full_text: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties. The policies shall be reviewed at planned intervals or if significant changes occur to ensure their continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness. Policies should address the organization's approach to managing information security objectives.

controls.title: Information Security Roles and Responsibilities
controls.description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated. All information security responsibilities shall be clearly defined and allocated to ensure proper implementation and operation of the ISMS.
controls.full_text: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organization's needs. This includes defining responsibilities for the protection of individual assets and for carrying out specific information security processes. Responsibilities for information security risk management activities and acceptance of residual risks shall be defined. The allocation shall be documented and communicated.

controls.title: Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated to reduce opportunities for unauthorized or unintentional modification or misuse of the organization's assets.
controls.full_text: Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility shall be segregated to reduce opportunities for unauthorized or unintentional modification or misuse of the organization's assets. No single person should be able to access, modify, or use assets without authorization or detection. The initiation of an event should be separated from its authorization. The possibility of collusion should be considered in designing segregation controls.
evidence_requirements: Access control configurations showing SoD enforcement
Compensating controls documentation where SoD cannot be achieved

controls.title: Management Responsibilities
controls.description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy and topic-specific policies and procedures of the organization.
controls.full_text: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy and topic-specific policies and procedures of the organization. Management responsibilities include ensuring that personnel are properly briefed on their information security roles before being granted access, are provided with guidelines stating information security expectations, achieve a level of awareness relevant to their roles, and conform to the terms and conditions of employment.
evidence_requirements: Terms and conditions of employment with security clauses

controls.title: Contact with Authorities
controls.description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities such as law enforcement, regulatory bodies, and supervisory authorities.
controls.full_text: Appropriate contacts with relevant authorities shall be maintained. The organization should have procedures in place that specify when and by whom authorities should be contacted (e.g., law enforcement, fire departments, supervisory authorities) and how identified information security incidents should be reported in a timely manner. Contact with authorities is also relevant for incident response, business continuity, and compliance with legal and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: Contact with Special Interest Groups
controls.description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.
controls.full_text: Appropriate contacts with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations shall be maintained. Membership in special interest groups or forums should be considered as a means to receive early warnings of alerts, advisories, and patches. These contacts provide access to specialized information security advice and serve as liaison points for information security incident coordination.
evidence_requirements: List of relevant special interest groups and forums

controls.title: Threat Intelligence
controls.description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analyzed to produce threat intelligence for the organization.
controls.full_text: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analyzed to produce threat intelligence. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. The organization should establish processes for gathering, analyzing, and disseminating threat intelligence from multiple sources including government agencies, industry groups, vendors, and commercial threat feeds. Threat intelligence should be integrated into risk management and security operations processes.
evidence_requirements: Threat intelligence sources and feeds documentation
Threat intelligence integration into risk assessments

controls.title: Information Security in Project Management
controls.description: Information security shall be integrated into project management regardless of the type of the project.
controls.full_text: Information security shall be integrated into project management to ensure information security risks are addressed as part of project management. This applies to any type of project regardless of its nature. Project management methodologies should require that information security objectives are identified, information security risks are assessed and treated, information security requirements and controls are incorporated into project deliverables, and information security is reviewed at defined project milestones.
evidence_requirements: Project management methodology with security integration

controls.title: Inventory of Information and Other Associated Assets
controls.description: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained.
controls.full_text: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained. The inventory should include all information assets necessary for the organization to operate and achieve its objectives. Asset owners shall be assigned responsibility for the proper management of assets throughout their lifecycle. The inventory should be accurate, up to date, consistent, and aligned with other inventories.

controls.title: Acceptable Use of Information and Other Associated Assets
controls.description: Rules for the acceptable use of information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented, and implemented.
controls.full_text: Rules for the acceptable use of information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented, and implemented. Personnel and external party users using or having access to the organization's information and other associated assets shall be made aware of the information security requirements for use. They should be responsible for their use of any information processing resources and any such use carried out under their responsibility.

controls.title: Return of Assets
controls.description: Personnel and other interested parties as appropriate shall return all the organization's assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract, or agreement.
controls.full_text: All organizational assets in the possession of personnel and other interested parties shall be returned upon change or termination of their employment, contract, or agreement. The termination process should be formalized to include the return of all previously issued physical and electronic assets. Where personnel or external party users use personal equipment, procedures should ensure that all relevant information is transferred to the organization and securely deleted from the equipment.
evidence_requirements: Exit interview records confirming asset return

controls.title: Classification of Information
controls.description: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organization based on confidentiality, integrity, availability, and relevant interested party requirements.
controls.full_text: Information shall be classified in terms of legal requirements, value, criticality, and sensitivity to unauthorized disclosure or modification. The classification scheme should include conventions for classification and criteria for review of classification over time. The level of protection should be assessed by analyzing confidentiality, integrity, and availability requirements and any other requirements for the information considered, taking account of business needs for sharing or restricting information.

controls.title: Labelling of Information
controls.description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization.
controls.full_text: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization. Procedures for information labelling should cover information and associated assets in physical and electronic formats. The labelling should reflect the classification scheme established. Labels should be easily recognizable and managed throughout the information lifecycle.

controls.title: Information Transfer
controls.description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties.
controls.full_text: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties. Rules, procedures, and agreements should address the protection of transferred information from interception, unauthorized access, copying, modification, misrouting, and destruction. Procedures and controls to protect information transfer should reflect the classification of information involved.

controls.title: Access Control
controls.description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.
controls.full_text: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements. Access control rules should consider the business application requirements and be consistent with the applicable access control policy. Access control should take into account the principle of least privilege and need-to-know, with appropriate segregation of duties.

controls.title: Identity Management
controls.description: The full lifecycle of identities shall be managed, including registration, provisioning, administration, and deprovisioning of user identities.
controls.full_text: The full lifecycle of identities shall be managed. This includes the initial registration and provisioning of unique user identifiers, the administration and maintenance of identity information, and the timely deprovisioning or removal of identities that are no longer required. The identity management process should ensure that each user has a unique identifier and that shared identities are only approved where operationally necessary.

controls.title: Authentication Information
controls.description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information.
controls.full_text: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process. Users should be required to follow good security practices in the selection and use of passwords. Temporary authentication information should be unique to an individual and should not be guessable. Users should acknowledge receipt of authentication information. Default vendor authentication information should be altered after installation.

controls.title: Access Rights
controls.description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified, and removed in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.
controls.full_text: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified, and removed in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control. Access rights should be aligned with defined access control policies and rules. A formal user access provisioning process should be implemented to assign or revoke access rights for all user types to all systems and services. Access rights of users should be reviewed at regular intervals.

controls.title: Information Security in Supplier Relationships
controls.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services.
controls.full_text: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services. The organization should identify and impose information security requirements for mitigating the risks associated with supplier's access to the organization's information and information processing facilities. These requirements should be agreed with each supplier that may access, process, store, communicate, or provide IT infrastructure components for the organization's information.

controls.title: Addressing Information Security Within Supplier Agreements
controls.description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.
controls.full_text: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship. Agreements with suppliers should include requirements to address the information security risks associated with information and communications technology services and product supply chain. The agreement should include obligations for the supplier to report information security incidents and vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Managing Information Security in the ICT Supply Chain
controls.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented for managing information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.
controls.full_text: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented for managing information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. Requirements relating to information security should be included in agreements with ICT suppliers. The ICT supply chain includes cloud computing services, outsourced development, hardware and software suppliers, and managed service providers.
evidence_requirements: ICT supply chain risk management policy

controls.title: Monitoring, Review, and Change Management of Supplier Services
controls.description: The organization shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate, and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.
controls.full_text: The organization shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate, and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery. Monitoring and review of supplier services should ensure that information security terms and conditions of agreements are adhered to, and that information security incidents and problems are managed properly. This should involve a service management relationship between the organization and the supplier.
evidence_requirements: Change management records for supplier services

controls.title: Information Security for Use of Cloud Services
controls.description: Processes for acquisition, use, management, and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements.
controls.full_text: Processes for acquisition, use, management, and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. The organization should define requirements for cloud service security including data location, service level agreements, incident management, exit strategy, and encryption requirements. Shared responsibilities between the cloud service provider and the organization should be clearly defined.

controls.title: Information Security Incident Management Planning and Preparation
controls.description: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing, and communicating information security incident management processes, roles, and responsibilities.
controls.full_text: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing, and communicating information security incident management processes, roles, and responsibilities. This includes establishing incident response procedures, defining reporting channels, establishing incident response teams, and preparing evidence collection and preservation procedures for potential legal proceedings or disciplinary actions.
evidence_requirements: Incident response team charter and roster

controls.title: Assessment and Decision on Information Security Events
controls.description: The organization shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorized as information security incidents.
controls.full_text: The organization shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorized as information security incidents. Each information security event should be assessed and a decision made on whether it meets the agreed definition of an information security incident. The assessment should use an agreed classification scale to determine priority and resource allocation. Events should be correlated and analyzed to identify patterns.

controls.title: Response to Information Security Incidents
controls.description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.
controls.full_text: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures. The response should include containment of the incident, collection and preservation of evidence, escalation as needed, communication to internal and external stakeholders, documentation of all response activities, and analysis for determining the root cause. Response activities should be logged for subsequent analysis.

controls.title: Learning from Information Security Incidents
controls.description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.
controls.full_text: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls. Mechanisms should be in place to enable the types, volumes, and costs of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored. Lessons learned from incidents should be incorporated into the risk assessment process, awareness training programs, and control improvements. Trends and patterns should be analyzed to prevent recurrence.
evidence_requirements: Updated risk assessments based on incidents

controls.title: Collection of Evidence
controls.description: The organization shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition, and preservation of evidence related to information security events.
controls.full_text: The organization shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition, and preservation of evidence related to information security events. Procedures should be developed and followed for evidence collection and handling that comply with legal and regulatory requirements. Evidence should be collected in a manner that ensures its admissibility and integrity. Chain of custody procedures should be implemented.

controls.title: Information Security During Disruption
controls.description: The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.
controls.full_text: The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption. Information security requirements should be integrated into business continuity management. The organization should determine requirements for information security and the continuity of information security management in adverse situations. Information security continuity should be planned, implemented, and tested as an integral part of business continuity planning.

controls.title: ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
controls.description: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained, and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.
controls.full_text: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained, and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. ICT continuity planning should include identification of critical ICT services and their dependencies, assessment of the risks to ICT continuity, development and implementation of ICT continuity strategies and plans, and regular testing and exercising of ICT continuity arrangements.

controls.title: Legal, Statutory, Regulatory, and Contractual Requirements
controls.description: Legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organization's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented, and kept up to date.
controls.full_text: Legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organization's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented, and kept up to date. The specific controls and individual responsibilities to meet these requirements should be defined and documented. Management should identify all legislation applicable to the organization, including legislation related to information processing, privacy, and cryptographic controls.

controls.title: Intellectual Property Rights
controls.description: The organization shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.
controls.full_text: The organization shall implement appropriate procedures to ensure compliance with legislative, regulatory, and contractual requirements related to intellectual property rights and use of proprietary software products. This includes software license management, policies on acquisition of software, awareness of intellectual property rights policies, maintaining proof of license ownership, and implementing controls to ensure maximum number of users is not exceeded.

controls.title: Protection of Records
controls.description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access, and unauthorized release in accordance with legislative, regulatory, contractual, and business requirements.
controls.full_text: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access, and unauthorized release in accordance with legislative, regulatory, contractual, and business requirements. Controls should consider the classification of records, applicable retention periods, permitted storage media, authorized access and handling, and the eventual secure destruction or disposal when retention periods expire.

controls.title: Privacy and Protection of Personal Identifiable Information (PII)
controls.description: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of PII as required in applicable legislation, regulations, and contractual requirements.
controls.full_text: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of PII as required in applicable legislation and regulations and contractual requirements. A data protection officer or equivalent function should be appointed. Privacy by design and by default principles should be implemented. Processing of PII should be documented including purpose, legal basis, retention periods, and data subject rights procedures.
evidence_requirements: Data processing inventory/records of processing activities

controls.title: Independent Review of Information Security
controls.description: The organization's approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes, and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.
controls.full_text: The organization's approach to managing information security and its implementation shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals, or when significant changes occur. Independent review should be initiated by management and may be carried out by internal audit functions, independent external parties, or an independent manager. Results of independent reviews should be reported to management and records maintained. Corrective actions should be taken when the independent review identifies that the organization's approach is inadequate.

controls.title: Compliance with Policies, Rules, and Standards for Information Security
controls.description: Compliance with the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules, and standards shall be regularly reviewed.
controls.full_text: Compliance with the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules, and standards shall be regularly reviewed. Managers should regularly review the compliance of information processing and procedures within their area of responsibility. If any non-compliance is found as a result of the review, managers should identify causes and evaluate the need for corrective actions. Results of reviews and corrective actions should be recorded and maintained.

controls.title: Documented Operating Procedures
controls.description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.
controls.full_text: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them. Documented procedures should be prepared for system activities associated with information processing and communication facilities, such as computer start-up and close-down, backup, equipment maintenance, media handling, computer room and mail handling management, and safety procedures. Operating procedures should specify instructions for detailed execution of each job.

controls.title: Screening
controls.description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis taking into consideration applicable laws, regulations, and ethics and be proportional to the business requirements, the classification of the information to be accessed, and the perceived risks.
controls.full_text: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis. Screening should take into consideration all relevant privacy, PII protection, and employment legislation. Checks should include character references, verification of CV/resume information, confirmation of academic and professional qualifications, independent identity checks, and credit/criminal records checks where permitted and relevant to the role.
evidence_requirements: Screening completion records for all personnel

controls.title: Terms and Conditions of Employment
controls.description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security.
controls.full_text: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security. Terms and conditions should include responsibilities for information classification, asset management, information handling, use of IT equipment, internet and email, remote working, and actions in case of disregard of security requirements. Information security roles and responsibilities should extend beyond the organization's premises and normal working hours.

controls.title: Information Security Awareness, Education, and Training
controls.description: Personnel of the organization and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness education and training and regular updates of the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies, and procedures as relevant for their job function.
controls.full_text: Personnel of the organization and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness education and training and regular updates. The awareness program should include activities such as initial orientation for new employees, regular awareness campaigns, specific training for roles with significant security responsibilities, and updates when policies change. Training should cover the organization's information security policies, personal responsibilities, incident reporting procedures, and consequences of non-compliance.

controls.title: Disciplinary Process
controls.description: A disciplinary process shall be formalized and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation.
controls.full_text: A formal disciplinary process shall be in place and communicated to take action against personnel who have committed an information security breach. The disciplinary process should not be started without prior verification that a security breach has occurred. The formal disciplinary process should ensure correct and fair treatment for personnel who are suspected of committing breaches. It should provide for a graduated response that takes into consideration the nature and gravity of the breach.

controls.title: Responsibilities After Termination or Change of Employment
controls.description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced, and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.
controls.full_text: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced, and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties. Post-employment obligations should include ongoing confidentiality requirements, return of assets, intellectual property restrictions, and knowledge transfer requirements. Non-compete and non-solicitation clauses should be enforced as applicable. Offboarding procedures should ensure access is revoked.

controls.title: Confidentiality or Non-Disclosure Agreements
controls.description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organization's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed, and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.
controls.full_text: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organization's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed, and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties. NDAs should address the requirement to protect confidential information, the expected duration of the agreement, required actions when an agreement is terminated, responsibilities and actions of signatories to avoid unauthorized information disclosure, and actions expected to be taken in case of a breach.
evidence_requirements: Signed NDA records for all personnel and third parties

controls.title: Remote Working
controls.description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed, or stored outside the organization's premises.
controls.full_text: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed, or stored outside the organization's premises. Remote working arrangements should address physical security of the remote site, communications security, threat of unauthorized access, use of home networks, insurance implications, and the provision of suitable equipment and furniture. Remote working policies should define conditions and restrictions on remote working, including approval requirements and security controls.

controls.title: Information Security Event Reporting
controls.description: The organization shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.
controls.full_text: The organization shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner. All personnel and contractors should be made aware of their responsibility to report information security events as quickly as possible. They should be aware of the procedure for reporting information security events and the point of contact to whom events should be reported. Reporting mechanisms should be as easy and accessible as possible.

controls.title: Physical Security Perimeters
controls.description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.
controls.full_text: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets. Physical protection should be achieved by creating one or more physical barriers around the organization's premises and information processing facilities. The use of the perimeters and the strength of each perimeter should depend on the security requirements of the assets within the perimeter and on the results of a risk assessment.
evidence_requirements: Building floor plans showing security zones

controls.title: Physical Entry
controls.description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points to ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed access.
controls.full_text: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points to ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed access. Access to areas where information and other associated assets are stored and processed should be controlled and restricted to authorized persons only. Authentication controls should be implemented, such as access cards, PINs, or biometrics. Visitor access should be supervised, and entry/exit times recorded.

controls.title: Securing Offices, Rooms, and Facilities
controls.description: Physical security for offices, rooms, and facilities shall be designed and implemented.
controls.full_text: Physical security for offices, rooms, and facilities shall be designed and implemented. Key facilities should be sited to avoid access by the public. Buildings should be unobtrusive and give minimum indication of their purpose, where feasible. Facilities should be configured to prevent confidential information or activities from being visible and audible from outside. Directories and internal telephone listings identifying locations of confidential information processing facilities should not be readily accessible.

controls.title: Physical Security Monitoring
controls.description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.
controls.full_text: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. Monitoring systems should include CCTV, intrusion detection systems, and alarm systems. Monitoring should be designed to detect and deter unauthorized access attempts. Monitoring data should be reviewed regularly and retained according to organizational policies and legal requirements.
evidence_requirements: Alarm and intrusion detection system records

controls.title: Protecting Against Physical and Environmental Threats
controls.description: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.
controls.full_text: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure, shall be designed and implemented. Physical protection should be designed and applied, taking into account the results of risk assessment. Natural and man-made threats should be considered including fire, flood, earthquake, explosion, civil unrest, and other forms of natural or man-made disaster. Suitable fire detection and suppression systems should be installed.

controls.title: Working in Secure Areas
controls.description: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.
controls.full_text: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented. Controls should consider that personnel should only be aware of the existence of, or activities within, a secure area on a need-to-know basis. Unsupervised working in secure areas should be avoided both for safety reasons and to prevent opportunities for malicious activities. Vacant secure areas should be physically locked and periodically checked.

controls.title: Clear Desk and Clear Screen
controls.description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.
controls.full_text: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced. Information left on desks and screens is likely to be damaged or destroyed in a disaster or accessed by unauthorized persons. Sensitive or critical business information should be locked away when not required. Computers and terminals should be left logged off or protected with a screen lock when unattended.
evidence_requirements: Clear desk and clear screen policy

controls.title: Equipment Siting and Protection
controls.description: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected to reduce the risks from physical and environmental threats, and opportunities for unauthorized access.
controls.full_text: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected. Equipment should be sited to reduce unnecessary access into work areas. Information processing facilities handling sensitive data should be positioned to reduce the risk of information being viewed by unauthorized persons during use. Storage facilities should be secured to avoid unauthorized access. Items requiring special protection should be isolated to reduce the general level of protection required.
evidence_requirements: Data center layout and environmental controls

controls.title: Security of Assets Off-Premises
controls.description: Off-site assets shall be protected taking into account the different risks of working outside the organization's premises.
controls.full_text: Off-site assets shall be protected. Security measures should be applied to off-site assets taking into account the different risks of working outside the organization's premises. Regardless of ownership, the use of information processing equipment outside the organization's premises should be authorized by management. Equipment and media taken off-premises should not be left unattended in public places. Manufacturer's instructions for protecting equipment should be observed at all times.

controls.title: Storage Media
controls.description: Storage media shall be managed through their lifecycle of acquisition, use, transportation, and disposal in accordance with the organization's classification scheme and handling requirements.
controls.full_text: Storage media shall be managed through their lifecycle of acquisition, use, transportation, and disposal in accordance with the organization's classification scheme and handling requirements. Media containing information should be protected against unauthorized access, misuse, or corruption during transportation. Formal procedures for the management of removable media should be implemented. Media that are no longer required should be disposed of securely using formal procedures.

controls.title: Supporting Utilities
controls.description: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.
controls.full_text: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities. Equipment should be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities such as electricity, telecommunications, water supply, gas, sewage, ventilation, and air conditioning. UPS systems, generators, and redundant utility connections should be implemented for critical systems.

controls.title: Cabling Security
controls.description: Cables carrying power, data, or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference, or damage.
controls.full_text: Cables carrying power, data, or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference, or damage. Power and telecommunications cabling carrying data or supporting information services should be protected from interception, interference, or damage. Cables should be protected using conduits or routes that avoid areas accessible to the public. Power cables should be segregated from communications cables to prevent interference. Cable access points should be controlled.

controls.title: Equipment Maintenance
controls.description: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity, and continued confidentiality of information.
controls.full_text: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity, and continued confidentiality of information. Equipment should be maintained in accordance with the supplier's recommended service intervals and specifications. Only authorized maintenance personnel should carry out repairs and service equipment. Records of all suspected or actual faults and all preventive and corrective maintenance should be kept. Appropriate controls should be implemented when equipment is scheduled for maintenance.

controls.title: Secure Disposal or Re-Use of Equipment
controls.description: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.
controls.full_text: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use. Prior to disposal or re-use, equipment containing storage media should be checked to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or overwritten. Controls should include physical destruction, secure erasure using approved methods, or encryption with secure key disposal. Certificates of destruction should be obtained.

controls.title: User Endpoint Devices
controls.description: Information stored on, processed by, or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.
controls.full_text: Information stored on, processed by, or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected. A policy on the secure configuration and handling of user endpoint devices should be established. Controls should include device encryption, malware protection, remote wipe capability, screen lock, software restriction policies, and patch management. Mobile device management (MDM) solutions should be implemented to enforce security policies.

controls.title: Privileged Access Rights
controls.description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.
controls.full_text: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed. Privileged access rights should be allocated to users on a need-to-use basis and on an event-by-event basis aligned with the access control policy. An authorization process and a record of all privileges allocated should be maintained. Privileged access rights should not be granted until the authorization process is complete. Requirements for expiry of privileged access rights should be defined.
evidence_requirements: Privileged access request and approval records

controls.title: Information Access Restriction
controls.description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.
controls.full_text: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control. Access restrictions should be based on individual business application requirements and in accordance with the defined access control policy. Access control mechanisms should restrict access to information, application system functions, and data based on the principle of least privilege and need-to-know.

controls.title: Access to Source Code
controls.description: Read and write access to source code, development tools, and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.
controls.full_text: Read and write access to source code, development tools, and software libraries shall be appropriately managed. Access to program source code and associated items should be tightly controlled to prevent the introduction of unauthorized functionality, to avoid unintentional changes, and to maintain confidentiality of valuable intellectual property. Source code should be stored in a secured repository with access controls, version management, and audit logging.

controls.title: Secure Authentication
controls.description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be established and implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.
controls.full_text: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be established and implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control. Authentication procedures should include multi-factor authentication for critical systems, protection of authentication information during transmission, and secure storage of authentication credentials. Authentication mechanisms should be appropriate to the sensitivity of the information or system being accessed.

controls.title: Capacity Management
controls.description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.
controls.full_text: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements. Capacity demands should be identified taking into account the business criticality of the system concerned. System tuning and monitoring should be applied to ensure and improve the availability and efficiency of systems. Detective controls should be put in place to indicate problems in due time. Projections of future capacity requirements should take account of new business and system requirements.

controls.title: Protection Against Malware
controls.description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.
controls.full_text: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness. Detection, prevention, and recovery controls to protect against malware should be implemented, combined with appropriate user awareness. Anti-malware software should be installed and regularly updated. Systems should be regularly scanned for malware. Procedures should address the protection of systems from malware arriving through electronic communications, the internet, and removable media.

controls.title: Management of Technical Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained in a timely fashion, the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities shall be evaluated, and appropriate measures shall be taken.
controls.full_text: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained in a timely fashion, the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities shall be evaluated, and appropriate measures shall be taken. A timely response to the identification of potential technical vulnerabilities should be ensured. Vulnerability scans should be performed regularly. Patches should be tested and evaluated before they are installed. A current and complete inventory of assets is a prerequisite for effective technical vulnerability management.

controls.title: Configuration Management
controls.description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services, and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored, and reviewed.
controls.full_text: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services, and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored, and reviewed. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. Standard configuration templates should be defined for hardware, software, services, and networks. Configuration changes should be controlled through the change management process. Configurations should be monitored and any deviations from the approved baseline should be detected and addressed.

controls.title: Information Deletion
controls.description: Information stored in information systems, devices, or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.
controls.full_text: Information stored in information systems, devices, or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. Deletion methods should be appropriate to the sensitivity of the information. Deletion should comply with applicable legal and regulatory requirements. Records of deletion should be maintained. Deletion should include information in cloud services, backup media, and all copies.

controls.title: Data Masking
controls.description: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration.
controls.full_text: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. Data masking techniques such as anonymization, pseudonymization, tokenization, and redaction should be applied to protect sensitive data in non-production environments and to comply with privacy requirements.

controls.title: Data Leakage Prevention
controls.description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks, and any other devices that process, store, or transmit sensitive information.
controls.full_text: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks, and any other devices that process, store, or transmit sensitive information. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. DLP measures should monitor and control information flows to prevent unauthorized disclosure of sensitive information. DLP tools should be configured to detect and prevent data exfiltration through email, web uploads, removable media, and other channels. Alerts should be generated and investigated when potential data leakage is detected.

controls.title: Information Backup
controls.description: Backup copies of information, software, and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.
controls.full_text: Backup copies of information, software, and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup. The backup policy should define the organization's requirements for backup of information, software, and systems. The backup policy should define retention and protection requirements. Adequate backup facilities should be provided to ensure that all essential information and software can be recovered following a disaster or media failure. Restoration procedures should be regularly tested.

controls.title: Redundancy of Information Processing Facilities
controls.description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with sufficient redundancy to meet availability requirements.
controls.full_text: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with sufficient redundancy to meet availability requirements. The organization should identify the business requirements for availability of information systems. Where availability cannot be guaranteed using the existing systems architecture, redundant components or architectures should be considered. Redundant information processing facilities should be tested to ensure that failover from one component to another works as intended.

controls.title: Logging
controls.description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults, and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected, and analyzed.
controls.full_text: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults, and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected, and analyzed. Event logs recording user activities, exceptions, faults, and information security events should be produced, kept, and regularly reviewed. Log facilities and log information should be protected against tampering and unauthorized access. System administrator and system operator activities should be logged and the logs protected and regularly reviewed.

controls.title: Monitoring Activities
controls.description: Networks, systems, and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.
controls.full_text: Networks, systems, and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. Monitoring should include real-time analysis of security events, network traffic analysis, system performance monitoring, and application behavior monitoring. SIEM solutions should be implemented to correlate events and detect potential security incidents. Automated alerts should be configured for critical security events.

controls.title: Clock Synchronization
controls.description: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organization shall be synchronized to approved time sources.
controls.full_text: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organization shall be synchronized to approved time sources. All relevant information processing systems within the organization or security domain should be synchronized with an approved time source. The use of Network Time Protocol (NTP) or equivalent should be established to synchronize all clocks. Accurate clock settings are important to ensure the accuracy of audit logs, which may be required for investigations or as evidence in legal or disciplinary cases.

controls.title: Use of Privileged Utility Programs
controls.description: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.
controls.full_text: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled. The use and installation of utility programs should be restricted and monitored. Utility programs should be identified and controlled. Authorization levels for utility programs should be clearly defined. All use of utility programs should be logged and monitored. Unnecessary utility programs should be removed or disabled.

controls.title: Installation of Software on Operational Systems
controls.description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems.
controls.full_text: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems. The installation of software on operational systems should be controlled. Only authorized and tested software should be installed on operational systems. A software installation policy should define what types of software installations are permitted and what are prohibited. Installation should be performed only by authorized administrators after appropriate testing and approval.

controls.title: Networks Security
controls.description: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed, and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.
controls.full_text: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed, and controlled to protect information in systems and applications. Controls should be implemented to ensure the security of information in networks and the protection of connected services from unauthorized access. Network management responsibilities and procedures should be established. Network controls should include separation of network services, intrusion detection/prevention, and secure configuration of network devices.

controls.title: Security of Network Services
controls.description: Security mechanisms, service levels, and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented, and monitored.
controls.full_text: Security mechanisms, service levels, and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented, and monitored. The ability of the network service provider to manage agreed services in a secure way should be determined and regularly monitored, and the right to audit should be agreed. Security arrangements necessary for particular services should include security features, service levels, and management requirements. The organization should ensure that network service providers implement these measures.
evidence_requirements: Network service agreements with security requirements

controls.title: Segregation of Networks
controls.description: Groups of information services, users, and information systems shall be segregated in the organization's networks.
controls.full_text: Groups of information services, users, and information systems shall be segregated in the organization's networks. Large networks should be divided into separate network domains. Network segmentation should be based on the risk assessment and the trust level, criticality, and sensitivity of information systems. Wireless networks should be segregated from internal networks. Network access between domains should be controlled using gateways (e.g., firewalls, routers) with appropriate filtering rules.

controls.title: Web Filtering
controls.description: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.
controls.full_text: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. The organization should implement web filtering to block access to malicious websites, inappropriate content, and potential data exfiltration channels. Web filtering should be regularly updated to address emerging threats. Web access logs should be monitored and analyzed. Users should be educated about safe web browsing practices.
evidence_requirements: Web access logs and monitoring reports

controls.title: Use of Cryptography
controls.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.
controls.full_text: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented. A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information should be developed and implemented. Decisions on the appropriate level of cryptographic protection should be based on a risk assessment. Key management should support the use of cryptographic techniques. Appropriate standards should be defined for effective implementation throughout the organization.

controls.title: Secure Development Life Cycle
controls.description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.
controls.full_text: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied. Secure development guidelines should be in place. Security should be designed and implemented within the development lifecycle of information systems. Rules for development of software and systems should be applied to developments within the organization. Secure coding practices, security testing, and secure deployment procedures should be defined and followed throughout the development lifecycle.

controls.title: Application Security Requirements
controls.description: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified, and approved when developing or acquiring applications.
controls.full_text: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified, and approved when developing or acquiring applications. Information security related requirements should be included in the requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing information systems. Security requirements should address authentication, authorization, input validation, output encoding, session management, error handling, and logging. Requirements should be derived from risk assessment and organizational security policies.

controls.title: Secure System Architecture and Engineering Principles
controls.description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained, and applied to any information system development activities.
controls.full_text: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained, and applied to any information system development activities. Secure system engineering principles should be established, documented, maintained, and applied to any information system implementation efforts. These principles should include defense in depth, least privilege, fail-safe defaults, complete mediation, open design, separation of privilege, least common mechanism, and psychological acceptability.
evidence_requirements: System design documents with security considerations

controls.title: Secure Coding
controls.description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.
controls.full_text: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development. This is a new control in ISO 27001:2022. Secure coding practices should address input validation, output encoding, authentication and session management, access control, error handling and logging, data protection, communication security, and secure configuration. Code reviews should be performed to verify compliance with secure coding standards. Static and dynamic application security testing should be conducted.

controls.title: Security Testing in Development and Acceptance
controls.description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development lifecycle.
controls.full_text: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development lifecycle. Security testing should include both functional security testing and security vulnerability testing. Testing should be performed in a secure test environment that realistically represents the planned operational environment. New systems, updates, and new versions should be thoroughly tested and verified during development and prior to acceptance and deployment.

controls.title: Outsourced Development
controls.description: The organization shall direct, monitor, and review the activities related to outsourced system development.
controls.full_text: The organization shall direct, monitor, and review the activities related to outsourced system development. Where system development is outsourced, the organization should obtain assurances that the supplier's security controls and practices are adequate. Contractual agreements should address intellectual property rights, code quality, security testing requirements, and right to audit. The organization should review and verify the quality and accuracy of outsourced development work.
evidence_requirements: Outsourced development agreements with security requirements

controls.title: Separation of Development, Test, and Production Environments
controls.description: Development, testing, and production environments shall be separated and secured to reduce the risks of unauthorized access or changes to the production environment.
controls.full_text: Development, testing, and production environments shall be separated and secured to reduce the risks of unauthorized access or changes to the production environment. The level of separation between development, testing, and production environments that is necessary to prevent operational problems should be identified and corresponding controls should be implemented. Software should be migrated from development to production through defined and documented procedures.

controls.title: Change Management
controls.description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.
controls.full_text: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures. Changes to information systems within the development lifecycle should be controlled by the use of formal change management procedures. Change management procedures should include assessment of potential impacts, approval of changes, communication of change details, testing and validation, rollback procedures, and maintenance of an audit trail of all changes.

controls.title: Test Information
controls.description: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected, and managed.
controls.full_text: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected, and managed. The use of operational databases containing personal information or any other sensitive information for testing purposes should be avoided. If personal information or otherwise sensitive information is used for testing purposes, all sensitive details and content should be protected by removal or modification. Data protection procedures should be applied to test data. Operational data should be removed from a test environment immediately after testing is complete.

controls.title: Protection of Information Systems During Audit Testing
controls.description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.
controls.full_text: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management. Audit requirements and activities involving checks on operational systems should be carefully planned and agreed to minimize the risk of disruptions to business processes. The scope of the agreed technical audit tests should be controlled and limited. Audit tests should be limited to read-only access to software and data where possible.
evidence_requirements: Audit testing procedures and scope documentation
